info.name: RTM Class Backend
info.description: # RTM Class API - Ultra-Strict Integration Guide RTM Class is a classroom learning backend for schools and tutoring programs. It supports authentication, role-based access, class management, forum discussion, material processing with AI, assignments and grading, blog CMS, analytics, and admin moderation. ## API Identity - Base URL: `{{BASE_URL}}/api/v1` - Swagger: `{{BASE_URL}}/docs` - OpenAPI JSON: `{{BASE_URL}}/docs-json` - Required custom header: `x-client-domain` - Content type: `application/json` - Auth type: Bearer JWT (`Authorization: Bearer {{ACCESS_TOKEN}}`) ## Primary Roles - `ADMIN`: platform governance, user management, moderation, admin blog operations. - `TEACHER`: class owner/manager, material and assignment author, grading, classroom forum participation. - `STUDENT`: class participant, assignment submission, forum participation, blog commenting. ## Core Domain Modules - `Auth`: sign-up, sign-in, refresh token, me/profile, password reset OTP flow. - `Users (Admin)`: list/detail/create/update/suspend/delete user accounts. - `Classes`: create class, join by class code, list class and members. - `Materials`: upload/list/detail material and AI output retrieval. - `AI Jobs`: queue transformation tasks, inspect job status, callback simulation. - `Forums`: thread/comment/reply/upvote within classroom context. - `Assignments`: timeline, create/publish, submit, list submissions, grade, gradebook. - `Analytics`: class dashboard metrics for completion/performance/risk. - `Blogs`: public read endpoints + admin CMS + comments. - `Moderation (Admin)`: delete/remove cross-module content. - `System`: health endpoint for operational checks. ## Standard Response Envelope All endpoints follow this response envelope: ``` json { "message": "Human-readable status", "data": {}, "meta": {}, "error": null } ``` ## Error Envelope ``` json { "message": "Error summary", "data": null, "meta": null, "error": { "code": "BAD_REQUEST", "details": {} } } ``` ## Common Error Codes - `BAD_REQUEST`: invalid query/body/path/header contract. - `UNAUTHORIZED`: missing/invalid/expired token. - `FORBIDDEN`: valid token but role/access is not permitted. - `NOT_FOUND`: resource or route target does not exist. - `CONFLICT`: duplicate/constraint conflict. - `INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR`: unexpected backend failure. ## Collection Contract Style (Per Endpoint) Each request description is intentionally strict and frontend-ready: 1. Access contract 2. Header contract 3. Path parameter contract 4. Query contract 5. Body contract 6. Frontend payload checklist 7. URL example 8. Request body example 9. Success response example 10. Error matrix ## Header Contract (Global) - `x-client-domain: {{CLIENT_DOMAIN}}` - `Authorization: Bearer {{ACCESS_TOKEN}}` for protected routes. - `Content-Type: application/json` except multipart upload requests. ## Query Convention - Pagination keys generally use `page` + `per_page` (or route-specific keys if documented). - Sorting uses whitelisted fields and explicit sort direction. - Filtering supports route-specific allowed keys only. - Search applies only on fields documented in each endpoint. ## Auth Flow (Recommended Testing Sequence) 1. `POST /auth/sign-in` (or `sign-up` first if needed). 2. Save `access_token` and `refresh_token` to Postman environment. 3. Call protected endpoints using Bearer token. 4. Use `POST /auth/refresh-token` when access token expires. 5. Use `POST /auth/sign-out` to revoke session refresh token. ## OTP Password Reset Notes - `forgot-password` always returns generic success message for security. - Actual OTP delivery depends on SMTP configuration. - For dev-only API testing, email sending can be disabled while keeping endpoint behavior. ## AI Job Flow 1. Teacher uploads/creates material. 2. Queue AI transform jobs (`MCQ`, `ESSAY`, `SUMMARY`, etc.). 3. Poll job status endpoint or process callback. 4. Read generated outputs from material output endpoint. ## Assignment + Grading Flow 1. Teacher creates assignment (`DRAFT`). 2. Teacher publishes assignment (`PUBLISHED`). 3. Student submits. 4. Teacher lists submissions and grades. 5. Gradebook endpoint aggregates class score view. ## Analytics Scope Analytics endpoints are class-scoped and designed for teacher/admin visibility: - Completion/submission rates. - Performance averages. - Difficult question indicators. - At-risk student signals. ## Moderation Scope Admin moderation endpoints allow hard deletion/removal actions across modules: - Class - Material - Forum thread/comment - Assignment - Blog comment ## Frontend Integration Rules - Use enum values exactly as shown in examples. - Do not send unknown keys outside request contract. - Always parse envelope fields (`message`, `data`, `meta`, `error`). - Handle nullable fields defensively. - Treat IDs as opaque strings (UUID format expected). - Respect role boundaries in UI (hide forbidden actions client-side). ## Environment Variables (Postman) Expected key set: - `BASE_URL` - `CLIENT_DOMAIN` - `ACCESS_TOKEN` - `REFRESH_TOKEN` - `CLASS_ID` - `MATERIAL_ID` - `AI_JOB_ID` - `ASSIGNMENT_ID` - `SUBMISSION_ID` - `BLOG_SLUG` - `BLOG_COMMENT_ID` - `THREAD_ID` - `COMMENT_ID` - `USER_ID` ## Final Notes This collection is intended as an executable API contract for frontend integration. If endpoint behavior differs from docs, backend contract must be updated first, then examples regenerated to keep frontend and backend in sync. ## DB Diagram

## Public Get Blog By Slug

**GET** `/api/v1/blogs/sample-slug`

### 1) Access Contract

- Endpoint can be called without Bearer token in this collection item.

### 2) Header Contract (MUST send)

- `x-client-domain: {{CLIENT_DOMAIN}}`

### 3) Path Parameter Contract

- No path parameter.

### 4) Query Contract

- (No query parameter in this saved example)

- Only supported query params should be sent by frontend.

### 5) Body Contract

- No request body.

### 6) Frontend Payload Checklist

- Use exact enum casing from API examples.

- Send UUID-like ids exactly in required fields.

- Do not send unknown properties outside contract.

- Always parse envelope: `message`, `data`, `meta`, `error`.

### 7) URL Example (Postman)

```text

{{BASE_URL}}/api/v1/blogs/sample-slug

```

### 8) Request Body Example

No request body.

### 9) Success Response Example

```json
{
    "message":  "Blog fetched successfully",
    "data":  {
                 "id":  "1fd41d26-c7f7-4f67-95db-93283f4f9404",

                 "status":  "PUBLISHED",

}
```

- `400 BAD_REQUEST`: invalid payload/query/path format.

- `403 FORBIDDEN`: token valid but role/access not allowed.

- `404 NOT_FOUND`: route param target resource does not exist.

- `500 INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR`: unexpected server failure.

request.description:
## Admin List Blogs

**GET** `/api/v1/admin/blogs`

### 1) Access Contract

- Bearer token required.

- Role access depends on class/resource ownership and module rules.

### 2) Header Contract (MUST send)

- `x-client-domain: {{CLIENT_DOMAIN}}`

- `Authorization: Bearer {{ACCESS_TOKEN}}`

### 3) Path Parameter Contract

- No path parameter.

### 4) Query Contract

- `page`, `per_page` pagination

- `search` searches `title | excerpt | content`

- `isPublished` in `true | false`

- `sort_by` in `createdAt | publishedAt | title`

- `sort_order` in `asc | desc`

### 5) Body Contract

- No request body.

### 6) Frontend Payload Checklist

- Use exact enum casing from API examples.

- Send UUID-like ids exactly in required fields.

- Do not send unknown properties outside contract.

- Always parse envelope: `message`, `data`, `meta`, `error`.

### 7) URL Example (Postman)

```text

{{BASE_URL}}/api/v1/admin/blogs?page=1&per_page=10

```

### 8) Request Body Example

No request body.
